article_title: Radiological Factors Affecting Cage Subsidence after Single-level Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion with Double Titanium Cylindrical Cages
label: Fig. 1
caption: Various methods of radiographic measurement and representative of both centering cages, which caused no subsidence, and offset cages, which caused subsidence.a) Radiographic measurements from lateral X-rays in the neutral position. The anterior, middle, and posterior points of the superior endplate on the upper vertebral body (VB) and inferior endplate on the lower VB are measured.b) The distance between bilateral junctions at each uncinate process is defined as “a.” The distance from the junction to the midline of the two cylindrical cages is defined as “b.” The centering at the anterior-posterior (AP) view is defined as the ratio of b to a.c) The angle of the inserted cages to the posterior wall of the VB is defined as the sagittal angle.d) The distance from the anterior wall of the upper and lower VBs as measured from lateral X-ray on postoperative day 1.e) The angle of the inserted cages to the posterior wall of the VB is defined—from axial CT performed on postoperative day 1—as the axial angle.f) The angle of the two cages on axial CT on postoperative day 1 is defined as the CT two-cage angle.g) Centering cages at the AP view on postoperative day 1 (49.78%).h) Offset cages at the AP view on postoperative day 1 (38.25%).CT: computed tomography

article_title: Clinical and imaging outcomes of self-locking stand-alone cages and anterior cage-with-plate in three-level anterior cervical discectomy and fusion: a retrospective comparative study
journal: Journal of Orthopaedic Surgery and Research
label: Fig. 1
caption: A 53-year-old man who had experienced numbness in both hands and felt stepping on cotton in lower limbs for 1.5 years was diagnosed as cervical spondylotic myelopathy. A Preoperative cervical lateral X-ray plain radiograph showed the alignment of cervical spine was mild kyphosis. B, D, E, F Cross-sectional and sagittal T2-weighted MRI showed degenerative disc protrusion and spinal cord compression at C3–C4, C4–C5 and C5–C6, respectively. C Sagittal CT reconstruction showed that no osteophyte compression was found anterior to the spinal canal. The patient who underwent three-level ACDF with stand-alone self-locking cages. G The X-ray examination showed the alignment of cervical spine improved significantly two days postoperatively. H X-ray film which was taken six months postoperatively identified that the alignment of cervical spine was well maintained, and the positions of cages were good. I At twelve-month follow-up, the positions of cages were still remained, whereas the lordosis of cervical spine is reduced

article_title: Clinical and imaging outcomes of self-locking stand-alone cages and anterior cage-with-plate in three-level anterior cervical discectomy and fusion: a retrospective comparative study
journal: Journal of Orthopaedic Surgery and Research
label: Fig. 2
caption: A 63-year-old man who had experienced numbness in both hands and felt stepping on cotton in lower limbs for 3 years was diagnosed as cervical spondylotic myelopathy. A Preoperative cervical lateral X-ray plain radiograph showed the alignment of cervical spine was straight and osteophyte emerged at the anterior edge of the vertebral body. B, D, E, F Cross-sectional and sagittal T2-weighted MRI showed degenerative disc protrusion and spinal cord compression at C4–C5, C5–C6, and C6–C7, respectively. C Sagittal CT reconstruction showed that osteophyte compression was found at C5–6 level. The patient underwent three-level ACDF with anterior cage-with-plate. G The X-ray examination showed that the alignment of cervical spine improved significantly two days postoperatively. H X-ray film which was taken five months postoperatively identified that the alignment of cervical spine was well maintained, and the positions of cages were good. I At fourteen-month follow-up, the lordosis of the cervical spine and the positions of cages were still remained

article_title: Comparative Study of Clinical Outcomes of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion Using Autobone Graft or Cage with Bone Substitute
label: Fig. 1
caption: A 45-year-old female diagnosed with spinal stenosis at C5-6. (A, B) Preoperative magnetic resonance imaging. (C, D) Postoperative radiographs. (E, F) Follow-up radiograph 12 months postoperatively.

label: Fig. 2
caption: A 47-year-old female diagnosed with herniated nucleus pulposus on C5-6. (A, B) Preoperative magnetic resonance imaging. (C, D) Postoperative radiograph. (E, F) Follow-up radiograph taken 12 months postoperatively.
